title: Why Forme launched a World Cup promotion hedged on Kalshi
description: Forme used Kalshi to hedge a World Cup refund promo, which helped lift the brand's menswear traffic on its site. The campaign is a novel example of how e-commerce brands could use predictions markets as marketing tools.

title: Ulta’s chief marketer on blending brand building and omnichannel marketing
description: Kelly Mahoney digs into how the beauty retailer is driving growth with a playbook that spans cultural partnerships, TikTok Shop, retail media and more.

title: Dollar Shave Club’s bet: AI makes agencies optional, not obsolete
description: Dollar Shave Club makes 90% of its advertising in-house. AI is coming for the other 10%.

title: Anthropologie Chief Creative Officer Richa Srivastava on Japanese stationery, chore jackets and her ‘body bag’ duffel
description: In the latest installment of "What's in Your Cart," the Chief Creative Officer at Anthropologie, Richa Srivastava, shares her personal shopping habits with Modern Retail.

title: Your ‘omnichannel’ media strategy is ignoring half the shopper journey
description: Commerce media's measurement problem isn't a secret. Brands know it. Retailers know it. And Fluent has the answer.

title: Primark leans on expansion for growth
description: But the Irish apparel retailer needs more than new brick-and-mortar stores to stimulate consumer demand, according to GlobalData analysts.

title: AI Visibility Scores Are Useless
description:
The best visibility metrics measure what consumers actually enter, not carefully chosen prompts.
The post AI Visibility Scores Are Useless appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Milani benefits from TikTok Shop sales as brand marks 18 straight quarters of growth
description:
Beauty retailer Milani Cosmetics outperformed its sales goal by more than 250% for a product in its first two weeks of selling on the TikTok Shop. That was the first time Milani Cosmetics used TikTok Shop to launch a product that it would then make available through traditional retail stores and channels. The product, Highly […]
The post Milani benefits from TikTok Shop sales as brand marks 18 straight quarters of growth appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: LiveRamp Launches Brand Campaign on Netflix as Publicis Deal Fuels Scrutiny of its Neutrality
description: LiveRamp is pitching itself as a trustworthy foundation for AI marketing as ad industry players cast doubt on its ability to remain neutral under Publicis’ ownership.

title: Why Febreze is taking on soccer stink via podcasts, experiences
description: The P&G brand’s “Can’t Wash This” campaign comes as World Cup fever gives way to the beginning of the MLS season.

title: #224: Fable 5 Is Back, Palantir CEO’s Explosive Interview, the Pillars of Business AI Transformation & OpenAI Offers 5% of Company to US Government
description:
Are enterprises blindly surrendering their competitive edge to frontier AI labs? 

In Episode 224, hosts Paul Roetzer and Mike Kaput dissect the urgent power shift between AI developers, regulators, and the businesses that rely on them. We unpack the return of Claude Fable 5, Palantir CEO Alex Karp’s fiery warning on data ownership, and a new 8-pillar framework to safeguard your company's AI transformation. Finally, we cover OpenAI's massive new government pitch, Meta's internal struggles, and the harsh realities of AI's impact on the job market.

Show Notes: Access the show notes and show links here

AI-Pulse Survey: Fill out this week’s AI-Pulse Survey here.

00:51:48 — OpenAI Offers the US Government a 5% Stake

00:56:03 — OpenAI’s Inference Breakthrough

01:00:03 — AI Jobs Data Whiplash

01:09:35 — M

title: Dentsu strikes Meta deal to build plumbing for mass influencer activation
description: Top CMOs are assembling armies of creators, but many lack the infrastructure required to get the most out of them. A deal between Dentsu and Meta aims to fix that problem.

title: Ad Tech Briefing: Corporate America finally buys into soccer as a mainstream sport; shadier elements of the ad industry are obliging
description: The FIFA World Cup 2026, co-hosted across North America for the first time, has spelled out what some in U.S. media circles have known for some time: association football, or “soccer” to use local parlance, is now big in America, and it’s time for corporations to cash in at pace — one hydration break at […]

title: “Brands recognize the cultural dominance of podcasts”: global podcaster Mel Robbins talks AI, ad budgets and audience ownership
description: The global podcaster sat down with Digiday during her first-ever Cannes Lions — an event she now knows holds real value.
